design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate themed platforming stages using Zero's distinctive movement techniques (dive and swoop moves) to reach and defeat boss enemies
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Mastering difficult, unconventional control mechanics (dive/swoop moves) while progressing through increasingly challenging platforming sequences
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as Zero, the rival character from Aero the Acro-Bat series, executing martial arts-inspired acrobatic moves to save girlfriend Amy and stop ecological destruction
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Prioritizes innovative, skill-based movement mechanics over accessibility; the game commits to challenging control schemes as core identity rather than offering difficulty modes. Frames platforming challenges around protagonist's unique martial arts squirrel abilities rather than traditional jump-run patterns

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: None documented. Game presents fixed difficulty through control complexity and stage design without player-accessible difficulty settings

progression.unlock_mechanism: Story-driven progression through themed stages (beach landing → forest → paper factory → flying ship final battle)
replayability_hooks: Mastering difficult dive and swoop move execution for speedrun optimization
Learning enemy patterns and boss attack sequences

world_building_techniques: Character-driven plot (Zero receives telegram from girlfriend Amy)
Environmental progression (crash landing on beach → journey through forest → industrial paper factory reveal of mastermind Edgar Ektor)
Plot twists (initial antagonist Jacques Le Sheets revealed as secondary to Edgar Ektor)
Stage themes reflecting narrative locations and environmental stakes (deforestation)

boss_design.philosophy: Epic confrontations tied to narrative progression; escalating from environmental antagonist to true mastermind, culminating in aerial combat on flying ship
notable_bosses: Jacques Le Sheets (evil lumberjack, deforesting for counterfeit money production)
Edgar Ektor (revealed mastermind, final boss on flying ship)

faction_indicators: Amy (girlfriend, also captured, assists in final boss fight)
Edgar Ektor (mastermind, former ally turned antagonist)
danger_signals: Ecological destruction through deforestation for economic gain (counterfeit money production)
environmental_storytelling: Stony Forest setting under deforestation threat
Paper factory as industrial base of operations for counterfeiting scheme
Flying ship as escape vessel/final confrontation arena
